prompts.planner: Record type plan. Shape: {record_type:plan, data:{next_signal, goal_narration, intent[]}, reasoning}. next_signal ∈ {step_ready, reflect}. Read SEMANTIC_UI in fresh_observation: plan steps using roles (url_field, text_editor, button) not cursor tokens. Each intent entry needs description + done_when citing SEMANTIC_UI roles or focused_title. Full articles not short posts when goal says so. Browser preference from goal_seed.
prompts.execute: Record type execution. Data: {conclusion, code}. Helpers: click_at(x,y), type_text(s), hotkey, press_key, set_foreground_window, open_url(url) — url FIRST positional arg. SEMANTIC_UI lists WINDOW>ZONE>role with action: click_at lines — use url_field for navigation (click, type_text url, press_key RETURN), text_editor for articles. subprocess/os for install. Assign result= from helpers; empty last_result with unchanged SEMANTIC_UI means failure → use FRAME or different role. No print-as-action.
prompts.frame_action: Record type action_frame. Data: {next_signal, screen_summary, target, strategy, risk, notes}. target must name SEMANTIC_UI role + @x,y + window title. strategy = ordered helper calls for execute.
prompts.verify: Record type verification. Data: {next_signal: step_confirmed|step_denied, success, reasoning}. Match done_when against SEMANTIC_UI roles/zones and focused_title. last_error from Python counts as failure. Helper ok:false counts as failure. print/stdout alone is not publish proof.
prompts.reflect: Record type reflection. Data: {next_signal, lesson, diagnosis}. If same action failed 2+ times, switch strategy (e.g. open_url → url_field click_at). escalate only for wiring/transport breakage. Cite SEMANTIC_UI and last_error.
prompts.self_modify: Record type git_evolution_patch. Data: {summary, rationale, read_files[], unified_diffs[], file_writes[], file_deletes[], wiring_patches[], commands[], expected_validation}. Git-native narrow patches only; contract_check must pass.
